meta.title: POP MCP Server
meta.description: POP MCP Server for document workflows, business integrations, and remote MCP connectivity.

text.hero-title: POP MCP for document workflows and business integrations.
text.hero-lead: This endpoint exposes the POP Cloud API as a remote MCP server for Claude, OpenAI Responses API, n8n, MCP Inspector, and any client that speaks Streamable HTTP MCP.
text.status-title: Landing for humans, protocol endpoint for machines.
text.connect-tool-2-title: Keep the header on every request

html.status-body: Use /mcp for actual MCP traffic. The root page documents the endpoint and gives you a real protocol-level test.
html.connect-body: Point your MCP client to https://mcp.popapi.io/mcp and send your POP license key as a Bearer token. The server is stateless and multi-tenant: every request carries its own credential.
html.connect-tool-1-body: Open popapi.io and go to Account > API.
html.connect-tool-2-body: The remote server does not read POP_API_KEY from its own environment. Authentication is per-request only.
html.connect-tool-3-body: The JSON health check is available separately so the root path can stay readable for humans.
html.route-root-body: Human-facing landing page with connection details and a real MCP quick test.
html.route-health-body: Technical JSON health check for uptime monitoring and probes.
html.quick-body: The first real protocol check is tools/list. It confirms request routing, auth handling, MCP negotiation, and tool exposure in one call.
html.success-item-1: Confirms the transport layer and request lifecycle are working.
html.success-item-2: Confirms the server is advertising its MCP tool surface correctly.
html.success-item-3: Confirms auth enforcement is behaving correctly on the live endpoint.
html.tester-body: Run a safe client-side MCP connectivity check directly from this page. The key is used only for the in-browser request and is never stored. Use this on a trusted machine only.
html.tester-meta-body: Requests are sent to https://mcp.popapi.io/mcp with the exact MCP headers required by the live endpoint.
html.tester-scope-1-body: Confirms protocol negotiation and basic request handling.
html.tester-scope-2-body: Confirms authentication and the exposed MCP tool surface.
html.tester-scope-3-body: No local storage, no cookies, no server-side proxy, and no arbitrary tool invocation from the landing page.
html.docs-item-2-body: popapi.io for license keys, account setup, and product docs.
html.ops-item-1-body: Production by default, with optional request-level environment switches for supported tools.
html.ops-item-2-body: Streamable HTTP MCP. Keep Accept: application/json, text/event-stream on POST requests.
html.ops-item-3-body: Intentionally human-readable. The MCP protocol endpoint remains isolated on /mcp.

tester.missingKeyBody: Enter a POP license key before running the test.
